metadata.title:
GOV. JOHNSON
SEEING INLAND


EMPIRE TODAY

pages.raw_text:
Governor John A. johnson and
party are viewing the crops along
the Spokane & Inland Empire line
in the Palouse country this after.
noon, following a tour of the city.
as guests of the chamber of COM
merce this morning. A banquet
was given him at the Hall of the
Doges in Davenport's shortly be-
fore noon today by the chamber OF
commerce and with Mayor Pratt
and a committee from the chamber
of commerce, the party Immediate
ly left on the Palouse excursion.


This evening at Z o'clock the gov.
ernor and his party will be dined
by the Scandinavian Brotherhood
in the Hall of the Doges. He Is
accompanied on his trip to the Sc
attic exposition by his wife and
more than 40 eastern associates
and personal friends. His special
train did not arrive over the Great
Northern till nearly midnight last
night--too late to accept the greet
ings of the committee from the
chamber of commerce He IL still
reported a little indisposed be-
cause of an operation for appendi-
citis and is endeavoring to elimi-
nate late hours. His train ID In
charge of Adjutant General Fred
B. Wood of St. Paul. who acted al
his spokesman last night.


Several hundred business men
and women welcomed the big party
at the Great Northern this morning.
when automobiles furnished by the
chamber of commerce arrived to
take them out to view the city. The
governor is much pleased with the
city's prospects and with It's sub
urban beauty. Governor Johnson is
little behind his schedule, having
been expected in Seattle today. He
cannot get away from Spokane till
1885 tonight.
